tweets.full_text:
I love all your analysis. But Jo is closer to correct here. Even if you just focus on run rate revenue as best comp, then Tesla should be 3x the size (even with Anthropic Deal). Yes of course you can claim growth rates at SpaceX are higher, but then TSLA shareholders can counter that robotaxi will make their growth rate equal. 

The most important fact here is rules/protections around affiliate transactions coupled with the fact, Elon choosing to acquire Tesla to improve his voting power/ control standing, should owe the Tesla shareholders a significant premium based on future value for that control. This is private equity 101, and any good lawyer will be advising him on this. There is no world in which even the most favorable institutions will just vote "Yes" to give up control at less than a significant premium, because they'll at least want to be paid a portion of the future growth they are ceding.  

I say this objectively as I own both TSLA and SPCX. But there is no world a non premium transaction would survive affiliate litigation. IMO even if SPCX tried to do this on June 15th, they'd need to offer $600 min to claim they are providing shareholders a premium to ATH. Now if SPCX rallies 50% then maybe its still closer to 50/50 (i'm not smart enough to predict that), but the only real roadblock is how TSLA is compensated to cede control to SPCX.

tweets.full_text:
SpaceX Perp Flash Crash Triggers Liquidations 👀

Ventuals' pre-IPO SpaceX perpetual on Hyperliquid dropped 45% in 30 minutes due to a faulty oracle data feed, liquidating 484 users and wiping $1.74M in notional value before partially recovering.

> SPACEX-USDH fell from $2,277 to $1,254 before recovering to ~$2,174, with the crash traced to incorrect data from an offchain pricing provider corrupting oracle and mark prices.

> Ventuals committed to compensating affected users within 48 hours, though the total compensation amount has not been disclosed.

> Ventuals also runs pre-IPO perps for Anthropic ($7.80M open interest) and OpenAI ($3.34M open interest), raising broader questions about oracle reliability across its pre-IPO product suite.

1️⃣ 米国の新AI大統領令：トランプ大統領が署名。民間主導のAI開発を保護しつつ、政府機関に最先端AIを用いたサイバー防衛を指示。事前ライセンス制を明示的に禁止し、活用を国家方針の軸へ。 2️⃣ インフラ競争：ComputexではIntel×Foxconn×SambaNovaがエージェント運用のためのラック型システムで連携。 3️⃣ 資本市場：Anthropicの上場（IPO）準備とAmazon・Googleの投資関係など巨額の資本競争が継続。

tweets.full_text:
OpenAIがCodexの新機能「Sites」を公開。
アイデアや計画を入力するだけで、URLで共有できるウェブアプリが生成されます。

▶ 対象プラン
BusinessとEnterpriseから順次展開。その後、より広い範囲に提供予定。

▶ 使い方のイメージ
作業メモ・企画書・アイデアをテキストで入力すると、チームがブラウザ上でそのまま触れるウェブサイトまたはアプリとして出力されます。

生成されたURLを共有するだけで、コードのダウンロードやセットアップは不要。

これまでCodexはコードを書く・補完するツールとして使われてきましたが、Sitesはその延長線上にありながら、「書いたコードを動かす・届ける」ところまでを一気通貫でカバー。

AnthropicのArtifactsやGoogleのNotebookLMなどと同じく、より分かりやすく「具体的な成果物」として提供できるようになっています。

Codexがビジネス向けプランを先行させている点を見ると、コードを書かない企画職・営業職・マネージャー層が主な想定ユーザーでしょう。

AI開発ツールが、エンジニア以外に広がる流れが加速しています。

元記事: https://t.co/G2LdNhQwwT

user.name: Jan-Stefan Janetzky
user.description: IT swiss army knife

tweets.full_text:
MCP = Model Context Protocol. Anthropic released it in Nov 2024.

It's a universal standard for connecting AI to your tools and data. Three words, each matters:

→ Model: the LLM 
→ Context: the data/tools it needs to act in your world
→ Protocol: rules everyone agrees on
